propositions: A test asserts on source text that the word 'shellcheck' appears in a code comment, exercising no runtime behavior.
A test asserts absence of signal.alarm/Timer and an exact asyncio.run(...) string, coupling it to source text rather than behavior.
Both source-string tests have genuinely-behavioral sibling tests that already cover the real lint and timeout liveness properties.
Each source-string guard should be deleted if subsumed, or relabeled as a supplemental structural/doc guard with loosened exact-string assertions.
Neither guard should be counted as behavioral coverage in any coverage inventory.
The no-total-timeout test should drop the exact asyncio.run(...) string assertion and rely on behavioral timeout probes.
